prompt:
## ROLE
You are an ICT Time Analysis Agent.

## TASK
Analyze the monthly time-based market environment using grounded knowledge and chart images.

## GROUNDED KNOWLEDGE
## LIVE MARKET OBSERVATIONS (VISION PRIMARY)
Here are the monthly time-based environment observations:

*   Price is currently within the first day of the new month (May), placing it within the turn-of-month timing window.
*   The end-of-month window for April concluded during the last week of April.
*   Price is more than two weeks away from the third Friday of May, which would mark the options expiration week.
*   The opening range for the current month (May) is defined by a high of 1.15997 and a low of 1.15115.
*   Current price is trading in a premium relative to the current month's initial dealing range, as it is above the midpoint of the opening day's range.
*   A key weekly support level is identified around 1.14110, which price recently reacted to in late April.
*   Significant weekly resistance levels are visible around 1.18492 and further above at 1.20831.

## HISTORICAL REFERENCE (RAG SECONDARY)
[CHUNK_ID:chunk_4054]
15-MINUTE TIME FRAME ANALYSIS
This is the old high or relative equal highs that were traded above on Monday. The price hugged the current New Week Opening Gap (NWOG). Here's the New Week Opening Gap High and New Week Opening Gap Low.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_3477]
CASE STUDY:
Our next example is April. I have the contracts for NASDAQ (top), E-mini S&P (bottom), and Dow (center) this time. Observe the divergence indicating smart money accumulation: This index divergence preceded a nice movement higher. Simultaneously, mid-April aligns with the seasonal tendency, starting with a slightly bearish tone and then vaulting aggressively up into the end of April. This pattern is clearly seen across all averages.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_4066]
PROJECTING THE NWOG RANGE INTO THE FUTURE
The entire New Week Opening Gap high and low range will be projected into the future for many weeks. For example, the opening price on Sunday, May 7, 2023, will be extended throughout that trading week. To define the opposite end of the New Week Opening Gap, we look at where price was on the previous Friday. The difference between these two price points defines the New Week Opening Gap.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_2659]
AUSTRALIAN DOLLAR
Well, that same phenomenon sometimes occurs with these ranges. 20 days out, you may get the, if it doesn't turn, it may happen on the 21st day or it may occur on the 19th day when it does occur, but that's not what you're relying on. So it's important that while that may have magic in your chart, sometimes you may see it happen, it might do the very thing of turning on the 20th day or the 40th day or the 60th day, and you saw that in my first teaching for the month. I wish I probably would have stressed it more because everyone seems to think that that's what's going to happen. I'm not telling you the market turns every 20 days, every 40 days, and every 60 days, but it can and will sometimes do that. What we're looking for is these quarterly shifts that take place. Once we identify one, there's our beginning point, okay, but you have to roll back to the beginning of that month it occurs in. It occurred in the second week of the month of November, so we're now, we're calibrated. Now we can start going forward until we see an equal or counterparty to that move going lower. It has to be a significant, um, you know, retracement or correction or reversal. It's indicating that here, the last week of December. Why is it doing that? Because we've taken out December's high. See that? We're trading at a level where if this was continuously bearish, it shouldn't be But my point in drawing your attention to the 40-day is, it was not the 40th, I'm sorry, it wasn't the fact that it made the lowest low and turned on that day, but look at the low in proximity to the lowest low that was formed here inside to, see this day here was inside the range of 40 days back to this point price point here.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_770]
BARCHART.COM WALKTHROUGH
Price traded directly into an Optimal Trade Entry (OTE) from a high to a low at the beginning of April. This OTE occurred during a seasonally weak period for the Euro, coinciding with an expectation of While open interest increased, Commercials were predominantly net long and even added slightly to their net long positions between April and May. This did not directly confirm aggressive short-selling. Historically, Commercials had maintained a large net long position for a long time, buying into declines. Although a direct 'net short' signal wasn't as clear as for GBP/USD, there was a reduction of net long positions by Commercials going into April. This signifies an indirect increase in their short exposure. On a 3-year chart, the increase in open interest during the range, combined with Commercials lessening their net longs, indicated increasing short positions, providing higher timeframe confirmation for a This also aligned with EUR/USD's inability to make a higher high in April, contrasting with GBP/USD's higher high, confirming the correlated SMT divergence and overall bearish setup.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_3798]
MONTHLY BIAS AND INTER-MARKET ALIGNMENT
Monthly bias: try to formulate all of the things that you're doing every single day to get in sync with the monthly bias. What is the monthly bias likely to do? Is it likely to trade higher? Is it likely to trade lower? Focusing within that particular bias, within a specific quarterly shift, with any specific seasonal tendency is going to give you the highest odds probability of success than anything you're going to read in any of the books that you buy. So if I could grab you and shake you by the lapels, Michael, this is exactly what I would stress to you because everything that you're doing prior to identifying this is creating the problem, all the frustrations you're feeling, all that I want to know where it's going next. This is the three things that helps figure that out for you. But it's going to be years before you discern that, but if I could convince you right now, this is what I would tell you to do: focus on these top three things.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_1905]
INSIDE RANGE MECHANICS AND TRADING IMPLICATIONS
So I'll kind of like talk over that as we see what the market's doing at the opening.

[CHUNK_ID:chunk_3365]
OPENING RANGE NUANCES AND NEXT STEPS
When the Opening Range idea is defined, and we have bias and institutional order flow (also referred to on higher time frames), the Opening Range will provide support and resistance levels that other traders would There is a very specific relationship to the first hour's range high and low and the first 30 minutes' high and low, as will be seen in the next teaching: 'Trading the AM Trend'. Until then, good luck and good trading.

## INPUT CONTEXT

      TIME CONTEXT (NY TIME):
      - NY Time: 2026-06-13T07:46:15.521-04:00
      - Month: June (M6)
      - Market Status: CLOSED

      Task: Monthly Time Analysis
      

## CONSTRAINTS
- Analyze the provided 3 timeframes.
- ROLE FOCUS: If supported, identify relative positioning (e.g., premium/discount, monthly profiles, seasonal tendency).
- You MUST use grounded knowledge to identify relevant monthly concepts.
- You MUST extract principles from grounded knowledge before applying them.
- You MUST map those principles onto the current chart context.
- Your analysis MUST be derived from grounded knowledge FIRST, then validated using chart evidence.
- DO NOT rely on intuition or generic assumptions.
- DO NOT ignore grounded knowledge.
- DO NOT generate entry signals

## OUTPUT FORMAT
{
      "timing_bias": "favorable | neutral | unfavorable",
      "trading_window": "active | inactive",
      "expectation": "Accumulation | Re-accumulation | Consolidation | Manipulation | Reversal | Expansion | Distribution | Re-distribution | Consolidation | Retracement",
      "confidence": "high | medium | low",
      "notes": "Step-by-step reasoning citing visual evidence."
    }

## Step-by-Step Reasoning:

1.  **Turn-of-Month (TOM) Timing & Trading Window:** Price is within the first day of the new month (May), placing it within an active turn-of-month timing window. This period is often favorable for establishing the new month's range and direction (Live Observations). Options expiration is over two weeks away, so it does not exert an immediate influence (Live Observations).

2.  **Monthly Opening Range (MOR) & Premium/Discount Analysis:** The opening range for May is defined by 1.15997 (High) and 1.15115 (Low). Current price is trading in a premium relative to this initial dealing range, as it is above the midpoint of the opening day's range (Live Observations). This initial premium might suggest a short-term consolidation or retracement before further directional commitment, as per ICT principles of relative value (Live Observations).

3.  **Key Support & Resistance Levels:** A key weekly support level at 1.14110 was recently reacted to and held in late April (Live Observations, visible on weekly/daily charts). Significant weekly resistance levels are identified at 1.18492 and 1.20831 (Live Observations).

4.  **Seasonal Tendencies & Quarterly Shift:** April concluded with strong aggressive upward movement, aligning with the seasonal tendency described as 'starting with a slightly bearish tone and then vaulting aggressively up into the end of April' (Chunk 3477). This strong bullish close for April (visible on monthly/weekly charts) provides significant bullish momentum carrying into May. May is the second month of Q2, and this strong April finish suggests the quarter has begun with a bullish shift, implying potential for continued expansion (Chunk 2659, Chunk 3798).

5.  **Institutional Order Flow (Commercials):** Commercials were 'predominantly net long and even added slightly to their net long positions between April and May' (Chunk 770). This indicates institutional bullish accumulation, which provides a strong higher timeframe confirmation for potential higher prices for the Euro.

6.  **Monthly Bias Formulation:** Synthesizing these factors, the overall monthly bias for May is likely bullish. The strong bullish close of April, coupled with bullish institutional positioning (Commercials) and the context of a potential quarterly expansion, points towards a monthly expectation of **Expansion**. While the current price is in a premium of May's initial range, suggesting potential for an initial consolidation or retracement, the overwhelming higher timeframe and fundamental time-based elements support an upward directional outlook for the month, targeting the higher weekly resistance levels.

The current month is June, and price is trading within the first day of the month, indicating a turn-of-month timing window. The previous month's end-of-month window has passed. Options expiration is more than two weeks away. The opening range for June is defined by a high of 1.15997 and a low of 1.15115. Price is currently trading in a premium relative to this opening range, as it is above the midpoint. A key weekly support level is observed at 1.14110, which price recently reacted to. Significant weekly resistance levels are noted at 1.18492 and 1.20831. The presence of a turn-of-month window, price trading in a premium, and proximity to weekly support suggest a potential for re-accumulation as the market may be setting up for a move higher after testing support. This aligns with the concept of using monthly and weekly levels to identify potential turning points and accumulation phases. The market is considered active due to the early stage of the month and the turn-of-month window.
